behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure is most likely at module handoffs (manifest parser/resolver -> dependency graph -> vulnerability-check input). A pure unit test of one function can miss dropped transitive nodes or wiring bugs between components. No real external network/OS behavior is required to catch this class of failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner against fixture projects (e.g., npm and pip) containing known direct and transitive deps; use a local in-process fake vulnerability client to capture requested package@version entries; assert full expected dependency set is queried, including transitives.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Real failures often occur in HTTP serialization/encoding and API contract interpretation of version ranges. A mock can accept malformed or incomplete queries and hide the bug. Minimum reliable boundary is real HTTP behavior against a contract-faithful service endpoint.
behaviors.test_description: Start a local HTTP test server that enforces realistic API semantics for package + version-range queries; execute scanner end-to-end query path; assert request includes exact range syntax and returned CVEs are range-correct (no package-name-only matches).

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic ordering logic in one local boundary (comparator/sort function). No cross-module handoff or I/O is required to expose incorrect ordering rules.
behaviors.test_description: Call the sorter with mixed severities and tied severities with different CVSS scores; assert exact output order: Critical > High > Medium > Low, and descending CVSS within each severity.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Risk is in parse-update-serialize-file flow across modules; unit tests on updater logic can miss unintended rewrites introduced by serializer/file-writing integration. Local filesystem/serialization integration is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Use real manifest fixture files; run patch operation for one vulnerable dependency; diff original vs updated file and assert only target version token changed, with all other dependency entries unchanged.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real VCS/remote API behavior (branch push + PR creation + actual diff). Mocking repo/PR APIs can hide auth, branch, or diff-scope failures that only appear with real networked repository interactions.
behaviors.test_description: In a disposable real test repository, run scanner remediation path for one vulnerable dependency; verify remote PR exists and changed files/diff contain exactly one dependency version bump and no unrelated modifications.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump detection is deterministic local logic. One function/object boundary can expose the failure without I/O or cross-module dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit tests over semver pairs (including pre-release/build metadata) asserting breaking=true only when effective major version increases according to semver rules.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This is decision flow across components (semver evaluator -> remediation orchestrator -> PR client/output reporter). Unit-testing one function can miss orchestration bugs where PR is still invoked or flag is dropped.
behaviors.test_description: Run remediation orchestrator with a detected major bump using a fake PR adapter and real output formatter; assert no PR call occurred and scanner result includes explicit review-required flag/message.
